summary: Failure to auto-reimport glTF if vertices are moved inside of object's initial bounding box

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.1.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads)

### Issue description

If an object geometry is moved strictly in confines of its bounding box Godot won't automatically reimport it. You can work around it by reimporting manually if you're aware of this quirk, but it can be very confusing for a godot beginner trying to use blender for grayboxing since other engines handle such reimport with no problem

[More specifically](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/90659#issuecomment-2176338808) godot doesn't reimport the scene if only `.bin` changes but not `.gltf`

This issue is the exact same as #90659, most likely I just didn't test the PR properly so the issue was closed by mistake (I was sloppier at testing back then). In any case this is still present in 4.4.1, this time I'm attaching a video to better illustrate the problem:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec997826-638c-49f5-a658-ce7e7dd0dca9

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP, move some geometry and export the test collection or reproduce using your own blend file

summary: If “Autoplay on Load” is activated in the AnimationPlayer, animations of an imported mesh cannot be played by an AnimationTree whose node is placed above the mesh in the scene tree

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700 (20 threads)

### Issue description

I can't play any animations via the AnimationTree, if:
* “Autoplay on Load” is activated for a looped animation in the AnimationPlayer
* and the AnimationTree is placed above the animated mesh.
There are no errors or other logs.

If it's not a **looped animation** or the AnimationTree is **placed below** the animated mesh, it will work without problems.

This is the main scene configuration
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f4ecef36-c436-4f03-93b4-b39fd59d3746)

The AnimationTree
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/93ee62ea-feae-4842-97bc-395311ff5b03)

The used state machine
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d93fe7fa-bb06-4193-a27b-b26674fd0116)

I play the animations via code by
```gdscript
@onready var animation_tree: AnimationTree = $AnimationTree
@onready var move_state_machine: AnimationNodeStateMachinePlayback = animation_tree.get("parameters/MoveStateMachine/playback")

func _on_timer_timeout() -> void:
	if move_state_machine.get_current_node() == "Running":
		move_state_machine.travel("Idle")
	else:
		move_state_machine.travel("Running")
```

### Steps to reproduce

How I build the MRP:
* imported a mesh with animations and reimported an Idle animation and a Running animation to be able to loop
* activated “Autoplay on Load” in the AnimationPlayer for the looping Idle animation
* included the imported mesh as a node to the main scene tree
* put an AnimationTree above it, like in the pictures above
* try to travel states in the AnimationTree's StateMachine

If you deactivate "Autoplay on Load", change to a non-looping animation or place the AnimationTree above the imported mesh, everything works fine.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[new-game-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19796551/new-game-project.zip)

Behavior at editor scales below 100% is not affected to avoid impacting usability.

- See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/90843.

description.text:

Godot v4.5.dev2 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.11027.2001) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-13600KF (20 threads)

### Issue description

When saving a script A that `@export` another `@tool` script B in the scene that got some logic that call another class C that edit the `@export` variable from the script B, the nodes that use the script B in the scene get their script replaced with an embedded (saved in the .tscn file) copy of it

summary: Regression causing jittery canvas transforms on position updates

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on the cutting edge of the master branch.
- Not reproducible before commit `36a135ca62359e32ec8f0d20a4966bf517132eb4`.

### System information

Windows 10 - godot 4.5-dev compiled from source

### Issue description

I did some git bisecting and figured out that https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/104451 introduced a regression which could cause canvas transforms to not update for many frames. This would result in visibly jittery canvas item updates. I have a potential fix prepared here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105482

After that PR -- notice the white square jiggling as it tries to follow the sphere:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3aaa2ea9-c069-464f-91d5-144ee6bc4b44

Before that PR (and after the fix I have prepared already):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1562a6fb-fb93-445f-be6b-9298adfd2136


### Steps to reproduce

An easy way to reproduce this is to use a script like this on a canvas item that's like a color rect or panel, and set it to follow a node3d in the scene and while moving a camera around.
```gdscript
extends Control

@export var follow_target: Node3D

func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	position = get_viewport().get_camera_3d().unproject_position(follow_target.global_position)
```

summary: Properties of gltf materials are not cleared when reimported

description.text:
### Tested versions

Version: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6119) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12650H (16 threads)

### Issue description

When reimporting a gltf scene, the properties on materials are not properly overwritten when the gltf scene is reimported. Only after restarting Godot does the material get imported properly. For example, if a material had a texture, but it was removed, that texture will remain in the material until restarting. This also applies to other material properties such as doubleSided. Everything appears normal inside the advanced gltf import window.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a model with a texture in blender, and export it as a gltf and import it into Godot.

Blender   |  Godot
:--:|:--:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/613c460e-13d1-4c49-b104-3ffed3e29c6f) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/757266fd-38bc-42e6-bec8-c1feab9b8d5b)

Then, clear the texture in Blender, give the model an albedo texture instead, and reexport the gltf. The texture will now overlay the new albedo, even thought the gltf no longer contains a texture.

Blender | Godot
:--:|:--:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/feacb75c-b770-4493-8db3-1c1566458e0d) | ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dbfb23ee-7402-4b1a-8259-d5e6a3d62462) 

Restarting Godot will fix this issue. If the gltf scene is duplicated, the issue will be fixed on the duplicates, but not the original.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A - Restarting Godot fixes the issue.

summary: Terrible stutters when using Windows Dynamic Refresh Rate (DRR)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested on v4.4.1.stable.steam [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon(TM) 760M (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.13031.3015) - AMD Ryzen 5 7640U w/ Radeon 760M Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

When using the Dynamic Refresh Rate feature in windows enabled on one or more monitors in your setup Godot stutters every time the mouse starts or stops moving, if Godot is running on an high refresh-rate external monitor. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f8fa87d-b35a-408d-b8af-e722ef63ec47)
_100ms stutters make the game, or even the editor feel really bad._

What is Windows DRR? Windows DRR is a feature where Windows only uses the high refresh-rate (like 120hz) when the mouse moves, and when the mouse is not moving it uses something lower, like 60hz to save some power. To use drr, vrr needs to be enabled on a monitor. 

Where I noticed this issue:
My laptop has a vrr screen, and to save battery I use the drr feature. Godot never stutters on the internal screen, whether drr en enabled on the internal screen or not. The problems show up when I started using an external high refresh-rate screen. When using drr on any of the screens, Godot stutters on the external display, even if this display is not vrr. Godot does not stutter if drr is disabled on all screens. 

My laptop has an AMD gpu, I will test this on a Nvidia gpu in a few days if needed.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable windows DRR on any screen. (A recent version of windows 11 is needed and a vrr display.)
2. Open any project in playtesting and use the profiler to see the stutters everytime you start and stop to move your mouse. 

The screenshot is in Dutch, but it is the blue toggle under the refresh rate option in the windows settings.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b75d6cf6-1529-49b2-8b29-04024892585f)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any project will do. Even an empty scene.

[Web] Clean-up service worker and add `?no-cache`

description.text:
## Service worker cleanup
As it says, it's a global cleanup of the service worker in order to remove nested promises `.then()` and `.catch()` to use the (easier to read) `async`/`await` syntax. It was quite due.

## `?no-cache`
This introduces a way for developers to disable temporarily the use of cache from a service worker. Really nice when trying to debug something and you want to make sure that the service worker doesn't serve cache.

Simply add the `no-cache` search parameter in the URL of the navigated page (usually the one displayed in the browser address bar). It will ignore cache for every downloaded file of the session (until a reload or a page close, [see `pagehide`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/pagehide_event)).

Though, if you use `no-cache=no` (or any negative), it will ignore `no-cache`.

_Edit: As @roughbits01 suggested in the [Godot Developers Chat](https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/web?msg=PtWYvQkquBJjahPg2), it will REALLY help on mobile devices, as they don't have the tools enabled to properly remove a service worker without using a connected computer to debug._

summary: Setting an InputEvent device to 'all devices' sets it to -1, overlapping with DEVICE_ID_EMULATION

description.text:
### Tested versions

- reproducible in Godot v4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.4.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (14 threads)

### Issue description

My team and I were looking for ways to temporarily disable joypad input, and stumbled upon the idea we could set the device id on each InputMap's InputEvent to some high number so that they don't get triggered. However, we wanted to set it back to 'All Devices' after that and after printing a bunch of the properties we found the device id's had to be set to `-1` in order to work for all devices. I think this is defined here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3c0652699add1ca69ef18dc4408e044f485ab829/core/input/input_map.cpp#L42

And the place that enabled this functionality is here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3c0652699add1ca69ef18dc4408e044f485ab829/core/input/input_map.cpp#L168

The value of `-1` is mentioned in the InputEvent documentation under the constant `DEVICE_ID_EMULATION ` which has a completely different purpose: https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_inputevent.html#class-inputevent

Anyway, this was all very confusing and a bit of a search for us. I suppose it is best if the `ALL_DEVICES` and `DEVICE_ID_EMULATION` constants get merged into the same enumerator / constant, or that the docs are updated on this particular behavior.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a Joypad event to an action.
2. Print the device id's of all input events in each actions.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: RichTextLabel clips Theme modifications, such as Outline or Shadow

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in Godot 4.4.1.stable and Godot 4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.13031.3015) - AMD Ryzen 7 7700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

`RichTextLabel` clips Theme effects, such as _Outline_ or _Shadow_ that extend beyond its size.
This does not occur with `Label`, which does not adapt its size to these effects, but does not clip them either.

See here a RichTextLabel and a Label, respectively, with the same Outline and Shadow effects, however the first clips them whereas the second does not.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2eadcea8-8389-4b26-91a9-a406373e629d)

A behavior similar to `Label` would be expected from `RichTextLabel`, where the Theme does not alter the size of the Node, but it does not clip the former's effects.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a RichTextLabel and set the following properties in the Inspector:
- `FitContent` = TRUE
- `ScrollActive` = FALSE
- `Autowrap Mode` = OFF

Then, apply a Theme override with and Outline or Shadow effect, which will be clipped by the `RichTextLabel`'s bounding box.

[RichTextLabel_ThemeTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19763046/RichTextLabel_ThemeTest.zip)

summary: Script.get_global_name() doesn't work on inner class

description.text:
### Tested versions

Broken on:
- 4.5-dev2
- 4.4.1-stable
- 4.3-stable (first version with `get_global_name`)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - EndeavourOS #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Apr 2025 18:43:59 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (nvidia; 570.133.07) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

Calling [`get_global_name()`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_script.html#class-script-method-get-global-name) on an inner class' type returns an empty string.

Example:
```
class Example:
	pass

# `Example` can be used as a value directly, e.g. Example.get_global_name() gives an error. Hence why passed to function.
# `convert(Example, TYPE_OBJECT).get_global_name()` does the same
func get_script_name(script: Script) -> String:
	if script.get_global_name():
		return script.get_global_name()
	return ""

func _init() -> void:
	print("Script name: " + get_script_name(Example))
```

This prints `Script name: `.

If `Example` is in another file (with `class_name`), this prints `Script name: Example`.

Additionally, [`source_code`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_script.html#class-script-property-source-code) on inner classes return nothing.

#### Expected behavior

The expected behavior here is a bit unclear. This could either be the name of the class (which isn't technically global on it's own, so should likely be empty), or `Outer.Inner` (which required `class_name` on the outer class).

### Steps to reproduce

- Create new project, add a node, add script to node
- Add code above in script
- Run game, look at console

[test3.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19751905/test3.zip)

Enabling shadows on a SpotLight3D node in a scene removes shadows from OmniLight3D nodes in the scene. The shadows break both in-editor and in-game. Moving the OmniLight3D in editor causes the shadows to reappear until the SpotLight3D is moved again but they do not appear in-game.

summary: Fix GraphNode frag/vert port positions misaligned

description.text:
Issue conclusion: GraphNode port connections appear misaligned when switching between vertex and fragment modes, only realigning after zoom or layout refresh.

Investigation: The root cause was due to port positions being calculated before child layouts had been finalized, resulting in inaccurate Y positions for ports. The original implementation relied on Control::position.y, which was not yet updated during the initial layout pass.

Explanation: This fix computes vertical offset using titlebar and stylebox sizes instead, ensuring port alignment is correct immediately after layout, without relying on zoom-triggered re-layouts.

Resolves: #105232

summary: Add inspector support for typed property hint formats

description.text: Currently, the editor supports two methods of typing dictionaries and arrays, with the `PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE` hint (and the dictionary alternative), and the `PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING`. These type hints have different formats, and so need to be handled separately. That being said, the inspector currently does not accept the `PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE` and `PROPERTY_HINT_DICTIONARY_TYPE` formats, and so does not understand the associated type hints. This leads to nodes such as the GraphEdit's type_names property not having the proper inspector editor when creating a key/value pair (instead being the general object one). For reference; `PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING` is of the format `"%s:%s/%s" % [Variant::Type type, PropertyHint hint, String hint_string]`, while `PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE` is the human-readable class name like `"Texture2D"`. | Before; the add key and value are not typed: | After | | - | - | | | | This PR allows the inspector to receive this format, as well as adding the relevant documentation to the PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING and PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE constants. See also: #100926 A bit outdated but also: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/78656/files#r1736538595

summary: Update OS.request_permission (and related) descriptions to reflect reality.

description.text:
From a peek through the code, probably just some Legacy goop from the ever-evolving challenges Android likes to throw us. :-D

Contrary to what its previous description claimed, functionally, request_permission returns true not just when the permission has been granted. Pass it some junk, a 'normal' permission (even one without an entry in AndroidManifest!), or a typo, and - as request_permission is in essence actually a "!dispatched to Android" method - it also returns true! Quite misleading.

... fine for the plural method, request_permission**s**, because there's no room for bad parameters to get passed in there. Though this, in turn, suggests the original note about checking 'exports' for that method could use some elaboration for what that does; now offered.

Finally, it was originally unclear when, exactly, the on_request_permissions_result signal / signals might fire where the plural method initiated the requests; per permission, or all at once? It's <sorta> all at once - phew! - but with the caveat discovered, however, that any singular request_permission made whilst the OS dialogue is open is apparently just cast aside... even though our method returns the false value suggesting it was dispatched. :-/ Popped a warning on the request_permission description not to do something daft like that, then.

summary: LSP: Don't serialize empty documentation

description.text:
Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin/issues/842

When we send empty strings as documentation VSCode will still show a window for documentation but it stays empty. Those properties are optional in LSP so we can omit them if we were not able to resolve meaningful content.

This still doesn't solve the fact that we should be able to resolve the documentation in the context of the issue. But there are suggestions for which we will never be able to find documentation (e.g. argument options).

summary: Editor embedded game window: resizing window (`get_window().size =`) doesn't update viewport, causing crop or empty space

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 860M (nvidia; 535.183.01) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4710HQ CPU @ 2.50GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

I upgraded my pixel art game to Godot 4.4.1 and tested the new embedded game window, in both true embedded and floating modes, with different aspect ratio modes.

I use a custom script to automatically set the window size on game start, and also adjusts window position:

```gd
func set_window_scale(scale: float):
	var new_window_size := Vector2i(scale * get_native_window_size())

	var window := get_window()

	var previous_window_position := window.position
	var previous_window_size := window.size

	var new_window_position := previous_window_position + previous_window_size / 2 - new_window_size / 2

	window.position = new_window_position
	window.size = new_window_size
```

The part of the code that changes `window.position` never works, by design: I can see in the output log the message "Embedded window can't be moved." and I understand that.

However, the part changing the `window.size` shows no error, it just half-works and that causes odd results.

This is what the game looks like when not using the embedded game window (3 dots > uncheck Embed Game on Next Play), and the initial window scale is 2:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f93f990-0532-4bc7-95f7-727bf3ce7d36)

This is what it looks like in the embedded game window in mode 1 (size based on project settings):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f8c0233-c5bb-41a1-a782-fd253c1aef36)

Now if I resize the embedded game window a bit to force refresh, it will rescale the game down:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ee1ca9f3-13d5-4d13-9632-680b425286b6)

I totally understand that to properly scale the embedded game window, we'd need to resize the window inside the editor and that's not supported. My bug report is more about the fact that it does things half-way, scaling the game and cropping it, and without leaving a message like when trying to set window position.

Now this is what happens in mode 2 (preserve aspect ratio):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d59fea2f-c36a-493d-a740-2e756b0b72fd)

If I resize the window slightly, it shrinks back:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e6c49296-d9fd-4748-9d8e-ad25c5ea154d)

and the 3rd mode (stretch to fit) gives almost the same result, except with black bars.

When the embedded window is very big, mode 2/3 will leave a lot of empty space instead:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1df68acc-e872-4c34-8a7d-241598ec6f42)

Now floating window, mode 1 or mode 2:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c7dbdb76-b2b0-47ad-b65f-8a560a4f72b5)

Resize window a bit:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db17c660-e459-4016-bea9-3426e12f302b)

Floating window, mode 3:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cbc6e03b-e468-4e7a-88c6-f3a58edda1a3)

Resizing gives same result as in mode 1 or mode 2.

This time, I don't see why the floating window could not resize itself properly to fit the new viewport size. I still see the message "Embedded window can't be moved." so apparently it can't move itself either... but it's already floating, so it sounds weird.

If all of this is by design and our responsibility is to verify whether the game is embedded or not, I've already found I can check `Engine.is_embedded_in_editor()` (example directly taken from the PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/99010), so I'll do that for now.

However, for floating embedded window it does sound odd that moving and resizing is not supported - and even if it was fixed, I have currently no way to check if the embedded window is floating or not (would need some `Engine.is_embedded_window_floating()` method).

I realize I'll need a discussion > feature proposal to suggest to make at least floating game window resizable and allow to detect them. I'll see this later based on your replies.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new 2D project with those settings:

Display > Window > Viewport width: 426
Display > Window > Viewport height: 240
Display > Window > Stretch mode: Viewport
(keep scale 1, we need to script to change the game window size and modify scale thx to stretch mode)

You can add some Sprite2D with the default Godot icon texture just to see how things are scaled.

Then create a script:

```gd
func _ready():
	set_window_scale(2.0)

func set_window_scale(scale: float):
	var new_window_size := Vector2i(scale * get_native_window_size())

	var window := get_window()

	var previous_window_position := window.position
	var previous_window_size := window.size

	var new_window_position := previous_window_position + previous_window_size / 2 - new_window_size / 2

	window.position = new_window_position
	window.size = new_window_size
```

and add it to some node.

Then run the game.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I should probably make one based on my own instructions above later.
